creator: scheidell@FreeBSD.org
text:
State Changed
From-To: open->suspended


I am going to mark this port suspended. 

The Makefile is ugly, and won't install (first time) 
MAYBE it will install second time, but not first time 

See tinderbox logs 
===>  Checking if www/web-traceroute already installed 
mv /usr/local/www/cgi-bin/trace /usr/local/www/cgi-bin/trace.old 
mv: rename /usr/local/www/cgi-bin/trace to /usr/local/www/cgi-bin/trace.old: No such file or directory 
*** Error code 1 (ignored) 
cp trace /usr/local/www/cgi-bin/trace 
cp: /usr/local/www/cgi-bin/trace: No such file or directory 
*** Error code 1 

Stop in /work/a/ports/www/web-traceroute/work/web-traceroute. 
*** Error code 1 

See this in makefile: 

install: trace 
-mv $(BINDEST) $(BINDEST).old 
cp $(PROG) $(BINDEST) 
chown $(BINOWN) $(BINDEST) 
chmod $(BINMODE) $(BINDEST) 

create a clean patch, start with my patch I sent you, run portlint and resubmit it.
